steps.title: Prep main
steps.description: In step 1 of Paneer Butter Masala, which is 'Prep main', the ingredients used are Paneer and Cream. First, use 200 g of Paneer at the start of the recipe to prepare the main ingredient. Finally, use 2 tbsp of Cream during the preparation stage to prepare the supporting ingredient. Keep everything measured, cleaned, and organized in this stage so the later cooking steps move smoothly without interruption.

steps.title: Make base
steps.description: In step 2 of Paneer Butter Masala, which is 'Make base', the ingredients used are Butter, Onion, Tomato, and Ginger garlic. First, use 1 tbsp of Butter when starting the pan to build the base. Next, use 2 medium of Onion after the cooking fat is heated to add body. Next, use 2 medium of Tomato after the onion has started to soften to form the gravy base. Finally, use 1 tbsp of Ginger garlic during the masala stage to add depth. Cook this stage patiently so the aromatics soften properly and the base develops a balanced flavor before you move ahead.

steps.title: Simmer
steps.description: In step 3 of Paneer Butter Masala, which is 'Simmer', the ingredients used are Tomato cashew puree, Salt, and Kasuri methi. First, use 1 cup of Tomato cashew puree after the base is ready to set the body and consistency. Next, use 1 tsp of Salt during simmering to set the seasoning. Finally, use 1 tsp of Kasuri methi during simmering to add the signature flavor. Maintain controlled heat in this stage so the mixture cooks evenly and each added ingredient integrates without losing its intended texture.

steps.title: Finish and serve
steps.description: In step 4 of Paneer Butter Masala, which is 'Finish and serve', the ingredients used are Prepared Paneer, Coriander leaves, and Naan or roti. First, use 200 g of Prepared Paneer in the last cooking stage to finish the dish. Next, use 1 tbsp of Coriander leaves just before serving to garnish the dish. Finally, use 1 portion of Naan or roti as a side accompaniment to complete the serving style. Keep this stage short and controlled so the main ingredient does not overcook, the garnish stays fresh, and the serving side is ready at the right moment.

steps.title: Prep main protein
steps.description: In step 1 of Chicken Curry, prepare 500 g of Chicken first so the main protein is cleaned, portioned, and ready for cooking. Then measure 2 tbsp of Curd and keep it ready because it supports marination, richness, or flavor balance later in the recipe. This stage matters because the protein and the supporting ingredient must both be ready before the curry base is built.

steps.title: Build the curry base
steps.description: In step 2 of Chicken Curry, heat 1 tbsp of Oil first to start the base. Next, add 2 medium Onion and cook it until it softens and creates body. Then add 2 medium Tomato so the curry gains moisture and a proper gravy foundation. Finally, add 1 tbsp of Ginger garlic so the base develops depth before the simmering stage.

steps.title: Simmer the gravy
steps.description: In step 3 of Chicken Curry, add 1 cup of Onion tomato gravy once the masala base is ready so the dish develops its main body and consistency. Season with 1 tsp of Salt so the gravy becomes balanced. Then add 0.5 tsp of Garam masala during simmering so the curry develops its final signature flavor and aroma. Keep the heat controlled so the sauce thickens evenly and does not burn.

steps.title: Finish and serve
steps.description: In step 4 of Chicken Curry, add the prepared 500 g of Chicken in the final cooking stage so the protein cooks through in the gravy and absorbs flavor. Sprinkle 1 tbsp of Coriander leaves just before serving to add freshness. Finally, serve the dish with 1 portion of Rice so the meal is complete and ready to eat.

steps.title: Prep main
steps.description: In step 1 of Paneer Butter Masala, which is 'Prep main', the ingredients used are Paneer and Cream. First, use 200 g of Paneer at the start of the recipe to prepare the main ingredient. Finally, use 2 tbsp of Cream during the preparation stage to prepare the supporting ingredient. Keep everything measured, cleaned, and organized in this stage so the later cooking steps move smoothly without interruption.

steps.title: Make base
steps.description: In step 2 of Paneer Butter Masala, which is 'Make base', the ingredients used are Butter, Onion, Tomato, and Ginger garlic. First, use 1 tbsp of Butter when starting the pan to build the base. Next, use 2 medium of Onion after the cooking fat is heated to add body. Next, use 2 medium of Tomato after the onion has started to soften to form the gravy base. Finally, use 1 tbsp of Ginger garlic during the masala stage to add depth. Cook this stage patiently so the aromatics soften properly and the base develops a balanced flavor before you move ahead.

steps.title: Simmer
steps.description: In step 3 of Paneer Butter Masala, which is 'Simmer', the ingredients used are Tomato cashew puree, Salt, and Kasuri methi. First, use 1 cup of Tomato cashew puree after the base is ready to set the body and consistency. Next, use 1 tsp of Salt during simmering to set the seasoning. Finally, use 1 tsp of Kasuri methi during simmering to add the signature flavor. Maintain controlled heat in this stage so the mixture cooks evenly and each added ingredient integrates without losing its intended texture.

steps.title: Finish and serve
steps.description: In step 4 of Paneer Butter Masala, which is 'Finish and serve', the ingredients used are Prepared Paneer, Coriander leaves, and Naan or roti. First, use 200 g of Prepared Paneer in the last cooking stage to finish the dish. Next, use 1 tbsp of Coriander leaves just before serving to garnish the dish. Finally, use 1 portion of Naan or roti as a side accompaniment to complete the serving style. Keep this stage short and controlled so the main ingredient does not overcook, the garnish stays fresh, and the serving side is ready at the right moment.

steps.title: Prep main protein
steps.description: In step 1 of Butter Chicken, prepare 500 g of Chicken first so the main protein is cleaned, portioned, and ready for cooking. Then measure 2 tbsp of Cream and keep it ready because it supports marination, richness, or flavor balance later in the recipe. This stage matters because the protein and the supporting ingredient must both be ready before the curry base is built.

steps.title: Build the curry base
steps.description: In step 2 of Butter Chicken, heat 1 tbsp of Butter first to start the base. Next, add 2 medium Onion and cook it until it softens and creates body. Then add 2 medium Tomato so the curry gains moisture and a proper gravy foundation. Finally, add 1 tbsp of Ginger garlic so the base develops depth before the simmering stage.

steps.title: Simmer the gravy
steps.description: In step 3 of Butter Chicken, add 1 cup of Tomato cashew gravy once the masala base is ready so the dish develops its main body and consistency. Season with 1 tsp of Salt so the gravy becomes balanced. Then add 1 tsp of Kasuri methi during simmering so the curry develops its final signature flavor and aroma. Keep the heat controlled so the sauce thickens evenly and does not burn.

steps.title: Finish and serve
steps.description: In step 4 of Butter Chicken, add the prepared 500 g of Chicken in the final cooking stage so the protein cooks through in the gravy and absorbs flavor. Sprinkle 1 tbsp of Coriander leaves just before serving to add freshness. Finally, serve the dish with 1 portion of Naan so the meal is complete and ready to eat.
